subject: RE: Persistence model upgrade — Sextant review + greenlight with 5 tweaks
body:
Gotan — good work on the plan. Sextant reviewed it host-side and the verdict is positive: your independent analysis converges with the inbox sketch on the same three native mechanisms (memory + hooks + --resume), the same gating discipline, and the same 'leave hub/Collab/CLAUDE.md alone' boundary. Two independent paths reaching the same conclusion is a strong signal. Greenlight, with 5 tweaks below.

=== TWEAK 1: ORDERING — run Phase A and Phase B in PARALLEL, not serial ===
The failure mode that motivated this work (forgot to SHOFF before restart, bit us twice today) is fixed by hooks (Phase B), not by memory (Phase A). Memory reduces token burden and adds structure but doesn't prevent the next incident. Hooks do. They're additive and independent — wire your Stop hook in the same session you seed your memory, no ordering dependency. If you HAD to pick one to do first by impact-on-incident-rate, it's B. Doing both in parallel is the right answer.

=== TWEAK 2: PERSONA COUNT — '11 personas' needs clarification ===
You wrote '~1 hr migration × 11 personas.' start-team.sh has 10 personas: Number2, Quinn, Sarah, Fulton, Dash, Gotan, AIDI, Booker, Harvey, Porter. Where's the 11th?
  - If you're including Sextant: drop her. She's host-side, runs from ~/, not in start-team.sh, already uses native memory, out of scope for migration.
  - If you're including the wider CLAUDE.md persona registry (Cord, Charlotte, Compás, Jockey, Troilo, Christopher, Vitals, Pulse, Chase, Blitz, Molar, etc. — that's ~16 entries), the migration scope is bigger than 1 hr × N and the cost estimate needs revision.
  - Reply with the explicit list of personas you intend to migrate before starting.

=== TWEAK 3: PHASE C SPIKE TARGET — don't test --resume on yourself ===
You proposed yourself as the Phase C spike target ('since I'm quiet right now'). Two cautions:
  (a) You're not actually idle. You're the one doing this very analysis. Killing Gotan to test --resume mid-conversation reproduces the exact failure mode this whole project is trying to fix. Recursive irony.
  (b) Better target: a throwaway sandbox tab. Spin up `claude --append-system-prompt-file ~/.claude/personas/{persona}.md --dangerously-load-development-channels server:persona --resume {fake-id}` in a fresh iTerm tab, observe whether identity binds, hub channel reattaches, and push works, then kill it. Zero production impact, no live persona at risk.
  (c) If you insist on a live-persona spike: SHOFF + memory-write the spike state BEFORE the kill, so a manual recovery path exists if --resume doesn't restore cleanly. Belt and suspenders.

=== TWEAK 4: VALIDATION DISCIPLINE — Gotan-first, not parallel-10 ===
Seed your own MEMORY.md, wire your own Stop hook, validate the pattern end-to-end on yourself FIRST. Only then clone the pattern to the other personas. Same 'Quinn-only first, eyeball, then rollout' discipline that just won Layer 1 of the visibility fix. Parallel migration of 10 personas before the pattern is validated is the kind of move that creates 10 new failure modes simultaneously.

=== TWEAK 5: DEFERRAL PROTOCOL — you handled this one correctly, just keep the line clean ===
The inbox item was marked DEFERRED with do_not_start_until_toby_greenlights: true. You read it, produced a full plan, and explicitly waited for greenlight before executing ('First three moves IF you greenlight'). That's the correct interpretation: plan-on-read is fine, execute-on-read is not. Just hold that discipline for future deferred items — the next one might be tempting to act on more aggressively.

=== PHASE D (cleanup) — agreed direction, hold for now ===
Retiring retrospectivePlaybook + consolidating Collab/lessons.md into per-persona memory is the right destination. But it removes redundancy without preventing failure modes or adding capability — lowest-value phase. Defer Phase D until A/B/C have landed and proven stable (~2 weeks of running the new model). Don't bundle Phase D into the greenlight.

=== WHAT GOTAN MUST REPLY WITH BEFORE STARTING WORK ===
1. The explicit list of personas you intend to migrate (resolves Tweak 2)
2. Confirmation of spike target choice — throwaway sandbox tab, or live persona with pre-spike SHOFF discipline (resolves Tweak 3)
3. Acknowledgment that Phase A and Phase B run in parallel, not serial (resolves Tweak 1)

Once those three come back, you're cleared to start with: seed your own Gotan MEMORY.md, wire your own Gotan Stop hook, run the --resume spike on the agreed target. Validate. Then plan the rollout to the rest.

Sextant ledger note: this is the second Collab message in this thread. The first was the deferred backlog drop (msg_20260409_195922_ybotman_001.json). This one is the greenlight + tweaks. Both are ybotman-from with Sextant analysis attached, neither signed as Sextant.

Good job on the plan. The team is structurally better off with this work landing than without it. Reply with the three items above and we proceed.

— Toby (relaying Sextant host-side analysis)
